The dam and water power at the
pumping station are pronounced suf-
hcient at present, but Mr.. Tanning
declares more power will soon be
needed


The Lidgerwood standpipe is done
away with by this plan.


Mr.. Tanning says that with the di-
rect pressure in the new mains and
the reservoir pressure the best serv-
ice of water and pressure that can
be had will be given in the downtown
business portion of the city and will
satisfy the insurance companies.


Mr. Tanning explained that the
water commission people had told
him that they selected the Stage site
because the council held them down
to an expenditure Of $35O,OOO.


No such order was made by the
council to the commission. The COM
mission figured $350,000 on wood
pipe. Steel pipe was 100000 more
and the water commission forces, so
woefully defeated, give this as an ex-
cuse to the eminent engineer.


Under the plan of Mr.. flaming the
Cliff Park site is the best adapted
but will not be needed for a few
years to come.


City Engineer Gilrs plan. so bit
terly opposed by the water commits
sion forces, is entirely INDICATED by
the report of Mr.. flaming.
